subject: [PATCH v6 2/2] mm: kick writeback flusher for IOCB_DONTCACHE with targeted dirty tracking

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that the patch is generally good and suggested adding a Reviewed-by tag.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good. Feel free to add:

Reviewed-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

								Honza

2026-05-06.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's writeback mechanism to improve performance in the IOCB_DONTCACHE path. It introduces a background flusher that writes back dirty pages, reducing lock contention and latency. The patch also adds targeted dirty tracking using a dedicated WB_start_dontcache bit and per-wb counters to limit the scope of expedited writeback. This approach helps preserve I/O batching while improving performance in high-latency scenarios.

reviews.author: Andrew Morton
reviews.summary: Reviewer Andrew Morton expressed strong disappointment and skepticism about the patch, suggesting that it may not be the best solution to the problem, and pointed out that Sashiko has found additional issues.
reviews.raw_body:
Geeze, is that the best you can do ;)

Sashiko seems to have found more stuff:
	https://sashiko.dev/#/patchset/20260505-dontcache-v6-0-66463805dd6a@kernel.org

2026-05-08.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's writeback mechanism to improve performance in the IOCB_DONTCACHE path. It introduces a background flusher that writes back dirty pages, reducing lock contention and latency. reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledges that additional feedback was missed and plans to address it in the upcoming v7 patch release, indicating a fix is needed.
reviews.raw_body:
I saw those after I sent the last set and have been working on
addressing them. I've also found a couple more via dueling Gemini and
Claude reviews.

I'll have a v7 posting coming early next week.

Thanks,
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-05-09.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's writeback mechanism to improve performance in the IOCB_DONTCACHE path. It introduces a background flusher that writes back dirty pages, reducing lock contention and latency. The patch also adds targeted dirty tracking using a dedicated WB_start_dontcache bit and per-wb counters to limit the scope of expedited writeback. This approach helps preserve I/O batching while improving performance in high-latency scenarios.
